QUID for CMEMS OMIs: IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui Ref: 
Date: 
Issue: 
CMEMS-IBI-QUID-IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui 
3 April 2020 
1.1 
 
 
    
 Page 3/ 5 
Product name: IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui 
 
Indicator evaluation method: 
The Coastal Upwelling Index CUI is computed along the 
African and the Iberian Peninsula coasts. For each 
latitudinal point from 27N to 42N the CUI Index is defined 
as the temperature difference between the maximum and 
minimum temperature in a range of distance from the 
coast up to 3.5º westwards. The index is computed for two 
CMEMS products: IBI-MYP and IBI-NRT. 
𝐶𝑈𝐼𝑙𝑎𝑡 = max(𝑇𝑙𝑎𝑡) − min⁡(𝑇𝑙𝑎𝑡) 
The indes is computed from the folowing CMEMS 
products: 
• IBI-MYP: IBI_REANALYSIS_PHY_005_002 
• IBI-NRT: IBI_ANALYSIS_FORECAST_PHYS_005_001 
 
Scientific validation: 
The product IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui has been validated comparing its results with the classic Coastal 
Upwelling Index derived from wind stress forcing (Bak un, 1973). Both indices show a strong seasonal 
variability, however in the African coast displays positive index values almost all year round , which 
indicates an almost constant upwelling situation, whereas in the Iberian case this is mainly centred on 
summer time. 
  
Figure 2: Average monthly Upwelling Index in function of time and latitude,  derived from wind stress (left) and 
OMI_CUI derived from IBI-MYP (right) in the period 2002-2014. 
Comparing results obtained by the index obtained from wind stress forcing and IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui, 
a time lag (1-2 months) is diagnosed between both indices. This short lag has been observed in previous 
studies and can be explained by the inertia that the water bodies have to the atmospheric forcing.  
More details and full scientific evaluation can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State Report (Sotillo et al., 
2016). 
 
Figure 1: Representation of the masked area 
used to compute the CUI. Colors denote the 
mean SST derived from IBI-MYP product.

1.1 
 
 
    
 Page 4/ 5 
 
Product used for indicator evaluation: 
In order to monitor the results of the product , its results have been compared with an upwelling index 
based on the estimation of Ekman transport in perpendicular direction to shoreline (Bakun, 1973). This 
reference Ekman Upwelling Index is computed from wind stress forcing obtained from the ERA Interim 
wind data. 
 
  
 
 
Figure 3: Top panels: Coastal Upwelling Index in 2017 computed from ERA interim (left) and ERA5 (right). Bottom 
panel: Differences of CUI computed in 2017 from ERA interim and ERA5. 
 
 

QUID for CMEMS OMIs: IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui Ref: 
Date: 
Issue: 
CMEMS-IBI-QUID-IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui 
3 April 2020 
1.1 
 
 
    
 Page 5/ 5 
Assessment of impacts on the IBI Multi-Year Products Product: 
Since 2019  the atmospheric forcing is provided by the new ERA5 reanalysis instead of ERA interim 
reanalysis which is not produced anymore. In order to quantify the impact of this change on IBI 
reanalysis product, a sensitivity test on the IBI CUI OMI has been performed  for the year 2017. In this 
study, the CUI computed using the IBI MY product forced by the ERA-Interim and the CUI computed 
using the IBI MY forced by the ERA-5 reanalysis have been compared. 
Figure 3 shows the Hovmoller diagrams of the CUI indexes computed for year 2017 from the IBI 
reanalysis driven by the ERA interim and ERA5 atmospheric forcing. Seasonal cycles of both CUI indexes 
show a similar behaviour for both experiments. The higher CUI values occur from June to December in 
both cases. 
Paying attention to the differences in the CUI, shown in Figure 16, it is remarkable that bigger negative 
differences (CUI_IBI-ERA5 experiment higher than the CUI_IBI-ERAint) are located at 27.5ºN and 36.5ºN. 
The CUI index in these latitudes may be affected by regional coastal features such as the Cape St. Vince

catalog_entry.summary: This document is a Quality Underpinning Information Document (QUID) for the Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI) of the Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service, specifically focusing on the Coastal Upwelling Index (CUI). It details the methodology and validation process for the IBI_OMI_CURRENTS_cui product.
